title: Erdős #1052 companion infrastructure: unitary-divisor bridge lemma
informal_statement: Erdos #1052 (open): are there finitely many unitary perfect numbers? This packet proves only bounded companion infrastructure toward the known (Subbarao-Warren 1966) result that unitary perfect numbers are even -- NOT the open problem itself, and NOT yet the full even_of_isUnitaryPerfect headline theorem (which needs additional bijection/involution lemmas deferred to a future cycle). Defines uDiv n (unitary divisors of n) and proves the corpus's properUnitaryDivisors convention equals uDiv n with n itself removed.
informal_proof_idea: uDiv n is defined as n's divisors filtered by coprimality of d and n/d. mem_uDiv and self_mem_uDiv are direct unfoldings. proper_eq_erase rewrites both sides via Finset.mem_filter/mem_Ico/mem_erase/mem_uDiv and splits into the two directions of the resulting iff: forward direction is a direct one-line reconstruction; backward direction additionally derives 0 < d and d <= n from d | n to get the strict inequality d < n needed for Finset.Ico membership.
formal_statement_pp: theorem proper_eq_erase {n : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0) : ({d ∈ Finset.Ico 1 n | d ∣ n ∧ d.Coprime (n / d)} : Finset ℕ) = (uDiv n).erase n

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop; statement cross-checked against the sibling repo's already-kernel_verified proof of the same result

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only bounded companion infrastructure (a bridge lemma between two divisor-set conventions), not Erdos #1052 itself (genuinely OPEN: are there finitely many unitary perfect numbers?), and not even the full known Subbarao-Warren 1966 companion result (unitary perfect numbers are even) -- that headline theorem additionally needs ordProj_dvd_of_mem_uDiv, not_dvd_div_ordProj, filter_not_dvd_eq_uDiv_ordCompl, sum_uDiv_factor (a Finset.sum_bij' 5-argument bijection), and sum_uDiv_even (a Finset.sum_involution 4-argument involution) -- all present, already kernel_verified, in the sibling repo's source file, but deferred here since transporting their nested `.`-bullet case splits (bullets inside bullets, in the sum_bij' branches) requires substantial bullet-free restructuring that this cycle's budget did not cover; a future cycle should attempt them, recording any blocker in packets/frontier/erdos/BLOCKERS.md rather than re-discovering this scoping decision from scratch. Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 59a627f1, problem_version 41662057-906e-478a-9593-de5cf47322a7), submitted as a SubmitModule (1 def uDiv + 2 helper theorems mem_uDiv/self_mem_uDiv + root theorem proper_eq_erase), kernel_verified on the FIRST attempt. The only bullet hazard in this bounded scope (proper_eq_erase's `constructor; · A; · B` Iff split) was rewritten as `Iff.intro (fun ... => A) (fun ... => ?_)`, continuing the remaining goal linearly with ordinary tactics -- no bullets needed since a single-`?_` refine never requires case-split syntax. Independently re-verified in THIS environment's own pinned toolchain rather than treated as evidence merely because the sibling repo (F:\Github\mnehmos.llm-driven-proof-search.environment\ErdosProblems\erdos-1052\proof\Erdos1052_even_of_isUnitaryPerfect.lean) already reports it kernel_verified there -- per this repo's evidence policy. training.eligibility: quarantined per the default-quarantine policy for open_problem_related packets pending real external review (same policy as the #291/#399/#349/#494 packets).
